{"query":"IOC Olympic Charter amendments June 2026 individual discipline inclusion framework 2032 Brisbane","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1893,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.olympics.com/ioc/news/fit-for-the-future-new-olympic-programme-methodology-approved-to-shape-more-relevant-flexible-and-sustainable-olympic-games","title":"Fit for the Future: New Olympic programme methodology approved to shape more relevant, flexible and sustainable Olympic Games","content":"The IOC Session has approved the introduction of a discipline-based evaluation of the Olympic programme.
The new methodology will apply from the Brisbane 2032 Olympic Games onwards.
The methodology introduces a consistent and structured evaluation framework.
The IOC Session also approved a new framework concerning the disciplines which can be proposed by the Organising Committee of the Olympic Games (OCOG).
select from Recognised IFs that are either new to the Olympic programme or govern disciplines within sports already included in the programme – subject to defined limits and full cost responsibility."},{"url":"https://sportsin.biz/beyond-the-olympic-charter-the-iocs-new-model-changes-the-incentives-of-olympic-sport/","title":"Beyond the Olympic Charter: the IOC’s new model changes the incentives of Olympic sport - SportsIN","content":"That is why the debate should not be limited to what has been approved, but to what these decisions may produce over the next decade.
The Olympic programme is no longer just a list of sports; it becomes a permanent evaluation system in which each discipline will have to justify its place within a more flexible architecture."},{"url":"https://www.scmp.com/sport/other-sport/article/3358197/international-olympic-committee-president-initiates-reforms-regain-control-olympics","title":"International Olympic Committee accepts charter change on sports programme from 2032 | South China Morning Post","content":"The change means that ‌individual disciplines, rather ⁠than entire ⁠sports, will ‌be evaluated for ‌selection to ‌feature ‌in the Summer ⁠and Winter Games, as ⁠of the 2032 Brisbane ‌Games.
Also on Wednesday, the IOC accepted changes to the Olympic Charter ‌proposed by the executive board to reinforce the political neutrality of sport.
The amendments strengthen language emphasising that sport should be free from political ⁠interference, with one stressing the IOC’s role ‌in ensuring neutrality “at all times, free from governmental, cultural, societal or ‌economic pressure”."},{"url":"https://www.anocolympic.org/olympic-movement/ioc-session-sets-strategic-direction-to-ensure-that-the-olympic-movement-remains-strong-relevant-and-impactful/","title":"IOC Session sets strategic direction to ensure that the Olympic Movement remains strong, relevant and impactful : ANOC","content":"IOC Members, IOC Honorary Members, over 2,200 athletes and athlete representatives, IOC Commissions, International Federations, National Olympic Committees, Organising Committees for the Olympic Games, more than 100 Olympic Hosts and Legacy entities, the Worldwide Olympic Partners, Media Rights-Holders, media representatives and the IOC staff engaged in the discussion, with three key objectives:
It was announced that, for the first time in Olympic history, every athlete at the Olympic Games will be eligible for a new USD 10,000 “Fit for the Future Olympian Grant”.
A fund of USD 140 million per Olympiad has been set aside.
The first athletes to benefit from this initiative will be the Olympians who competed at the Milano Cortina 2026 Olympic Winter Games.
From the energy of Paris 2024, where the entire city came alive and millions shared unforgettable moments, to Milano Cortina 2026, the Games continue to show their power to connect and engage."}]}

GENEVA, June 24 (Reuters) - The International Olympic Committee on Wednesday approved amendments to the Olympic Charter proposed by ​the executive board, with the aim of reinforcing sport's political neutrality. The committee also accepted changes concerning the Olympic sports programme which mean that individual disciplines, rather than entire sports, will be evaluated for selection to feature in the summer and winter Games, as of the 2032 Brisbane Games.
